page: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features a single shared Intel x86_64 vCPU based on the Broadwell architecture running at 2.0 GHz, with 1 physical core and 1 thread. The system is equipped with 5.0 GB of system memory and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU providing 2 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage consists of a 90 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized performance characteristics. The instance achieves top-tier results in low-parameter LLM inference, delivering 9,931.92 tokens per second for prompt processing and 454.57 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M model. It also registers strong prompt processing performance of 211.23 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Conversely, general compute and memory subsystems perform poorly. The single-core stress-ng score is average at 1713.88, while the Passmark CPU score of 1849.31 and Passmark memory score of 1409.07 fall into the poor and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block read speeds at 13.71 GB/sec and write speeds at 10.61 GB/sec.

This instance offers a specialized entry point for GPU-accelerated tasks by providing fractional access to an enterprise NVIDIA A40 GPU. This resource density allows for cost-efficient execution of lightweight machine learning workloads without the expense of a full dedicated GPU. However, the shared single-core CPU, weak memory bandwidth, and poor multi-core performance represent significant tradeoffs. It is not suited for general-purpose web serving, database operations, or heavy multi-threaded CPU tasks. The optimal workload fit includes small-scale LLM inference, lightweight AI model prototyping, and basic CUDA-accelerated development tasks that require minimal CPU and system memory overhead.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring a single shared Intel x86_64 vCPU (Broadwell, 2.0 GHz), 5.0 GB of RAM, and a 90 GB NVMe SSD. It includes a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 2 GB of VRAM. Benchmarks show a stark contrast between top-tier performance in small-model LLM inference (9,931.92 tokens/sec for 135M prompt processing) and poor general-purpose compute metrics, such as a Passmark CPU score of 1849.31 and weak memory bandwidth. This configuration offers a cost-efficient entry point for lightweight GPU-accelerated tasks, though it is constrained by its shared single-core CPU and limited memory throughput. It is best suited for small-scale LLM inference, basic CUDA development, and lightweight machine learning prototyping rather than database operations or general web serving.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance with 1 shared Intel vCPU, 5 GB RAM, a 90 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 2 GB VRAM, optimized for lightweight LLM inference.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance with 1 shared Intel vCPU, 5 GB RAM, 90 GB NVMe SSD, and 2 GB of NVIDIA Ampere A40 VRAM.
tagline: A fractional GPU instance combining a shared Intel vCPU with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for lightweight machine learning and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 2 GB VRAM
Single shared Intel x86_64 vCPU at 2.0 GHz and 5.0 GB RAM
90 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance for small-model LLM inference
Limited general-purpose compute and memory bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight AI prototyping and CUDA development
